source.climateVerificationNote: No climate source URL is linked for this location.

desc: Darjeeling is a classic Himalayan hill station centered on tea estates, mountain viewpoints, and heritage rail culture. Travel quality varies strongly by season: spring and autumn are best for clear Kanchenjunga views, while monsoon months bring lush scenery but frequent rain, cloud, and road disruption risk.
hls: Monsoon months are lush but wet

todo.name: Tiger Hill sunrise
todo.desc: Go pre-dawn for Kanchenjunga views (and occasional Everest visibility).

todo.name: Darjeeling Himalayan Railway ride
todo.desc: Take the toy train segment for classic hill-station atmosphere.

todo.name: Happy Valley Tea Estate
todo.desc: Tour tea gardens and tasting rooms close to town.

todo.name: Mall Road and Chowrasta evening
todo.desc: Stroll the central promenade when mist lifts near sunset.

todo.name: Monastery and Japanese Peace Pagoda
todo.desc: Visit key spiritual landmarks on a half-day circuit.

todo.name: Day trip to Lamahatta or Takdah
todo.desc: Explore quieter pine-forest viewpoints outside town.

wifi.note: Central hotels and cafés are workable, but speeds and uptime can drop during storms, power interruptions, and landslide-prone weather windows.

prac.fltNote: Typical UK routing is Gatwick → major Indian hub → Bagdogra (IXB), then 3–4+ hour mountain transfer to Darjeeling. Return fares are often ~£420–£700 off-peak, ~£700–£980 in shoulder demand, and ~£980+ around peak holiday periods.
prac.lang: Nepali, Bengali, Hindi, English in tourism sectors
prac.visa: India eVisa is available for many nationalities; confirm current category and validity rules before travel.
alerts: Monsoon rains can trigger landslides and significant transfer delays on hill roads.
Cloud cover can obscure mountain views for days in wet periods.
Peak domestic holiday windows raise room rates and reduce last-minute availability.

sweet: March–May and October–November are usually the best windows for visibility, temperatures, and road reliability.
